rulings.Case Title: JERRY L. CATES et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 7, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Albay Construction Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that any of the insulation to which defendant¿s employees exposed Mr. Cates contained asbestos. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Cates was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. The court did not consider the declaration of Mr. Miles submitted with defendant¿s reply. (See San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308.) However, even if the court had considered his declaration, when read in conjunction with Mr. Cates¿ deposition testimony and declaration, it would merely create a triable issue whether defendant performed work at the Standard Oil refinery during the relevant timeframe. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY L. CATES et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 7, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Certainteed Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 4 (negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium), granted as to issue 5 (punitive damages) and off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues 1, 2 and 4, defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue of substantial factor causation. Dr. Bruch¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether exposure to Certainteed¿s asbestos cement pipe was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Cates¿ lung cancer. As to summary adjudication issue 5, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs have dismissed their false representation cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY L. CATES et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 7, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Swinerton Builders¿ motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Cates was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether the Johns-Manville pipe insulation to which defendant exposed Mr. Cates contained asbestos. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY L. CATES et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 7, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue of substantial factor causation. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Cates¿ asbestos-related illness. The court notes that on the exposure issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cates was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories and Mr. Cates¿ deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
